title: Sour Creamy Korean Beef Stir-fry
description: A quick-fix middle-eastern dish featuring a blend of sour flavors from white vinegar and creamy texture from sour cream, all sugar-free friendly.
instructions: Sous-vide the beef for 2 hours at 78°C (175°F).
Remove beef from sous-vide and let it cool slightly.
In a wok or large skillet, heat oil over high heat.
Add finely chopped jalapeno peppers to the hot oil and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add Korean chili garlic paste (gochujang) and stir-fry for another minute.
Remove the beef from its packaging, slice it thinly against the grain, and add it to the wok with sour cream and white vinegar. Stir everything together until well combined.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the beef is heated through.
Serve hot with rice or noodles.
